mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy path is still falling.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL year-over-year rate above the market's tolerance implies inflation persistence that keeps real rates elevated and pressures equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate crosses above its prior 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the event-study average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE and a simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS indicate labor-market deterioration that weakens demand expectations and raises recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration signal does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered months deliver non-negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS reflects stronger retail demand and current-quarter spending momentum, which should raise near-term earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales surges are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns on average, or if fewer than 52% of surge observations are positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above trend signal stronger construction activity and broader cyclical demand, but only when GDP growth is also firm; that combination should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is above its 6-month moving average and GDPC1 is rising on the latest release
failure_condition: Reject if the combined HOUST-GDPC1 condition does not improve 60-day forward returns versus either proxy alone, or if the average return under the trigger is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better capacity utilization, which tends to lift cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO months are not associated with positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal's predictive slope is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS acceleration with stable UNRATE signals broad employment gains without distress, which should raise consumption expectations and deliver positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if the employment-growth condition fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if negative/flat returns dominate triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate in a softening economy, measured by weakening PAYEMS and rising UNRATE, tightens financial conditions and should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and PAYEMS 3-month growth is negative or UNRATE has risen in the last 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the tightening-plus-labor-weakness trigger does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction adds no predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone.
